applicability_notes: Binary elementwise divide; second operand may be a tensor or python scalar. rounding_mode supports None/'trunc'/'floor' matching torch.div semantics. fast_and_approximate_mode trades precision for speed (bfloat16 NaN handling differs from torch, tests map NaN->inf). Supports preallocated output_tensor and queue_id.

placement_observations.source: unit_test: ttnn.from_torch(in_data2, dtype=ttnn_dtype, device=device, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: reduced stats tensor [{N},1,1,1] (std over dims [1,2,3], keepdim=True)
placement_observations.source: call_site: SD guidance rescale — std_ratio = ttnn.div(ttnn.std(noise_pred_text,...), ttnn.std(noise_pred,...)) followed by ttnn.mul chain
call_site: tt_latents = ttnn.div(tt_latents, scaling_factor) before vae.decode, inside trace capture (non-inplace required for tracing)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: row-broadcast reduction [{B},1,{S},1] (sum over dim=3, keepdim=True) — MoE top-k score normalization
placement_observations.source: call_site: topk_experts_scores_normalized = ttnn.div(topk_experts_scores, topk_expert_scores_sum) where sum = ttnn.sum(..., dim=3, keepdim=True) + eps
call_site: mean-pooling pattern — result = ttnn.div(ttnn.sum(emb*mask, 1), ttnn.clamp(ttnn.sum(mask, 1), min=1e-9))
